rationales.hook_strength: 'Rarely genetics' is a solid contrarian opener with a concrete 3-part diagnosis, though it lacks a sharp number or visual provocation to truly spike curiosity.
rationales.clarity: Each slide owns one lever (deficit, posture, hydration, sleep, exercise) with a single actionable takeaway and no filler.
rationales.audience_fit: Hits the 20–35 looksmaxxing audience exactly — phone posture, tired face, overnight fixes they can start tonight.
rationales.novelty: Fluid-pooling and tech-neck angles are fresher than the usual 'do neck rolls' advice, though calorie deficit and sleep tips are well-trodden.
rationales.brand_fit: Claims are hedged ('may help' equivalent with 'real — if minor'), no hype language, CTA ties directly to the three tracked metrics.
rationales.predicted_performance: High save potential from the multi-cause framing and actionable stack, but no dramatic visual hook or personal story limits viral share ceiling.
